qa_pairs.question: How do you configure a Slurm script to run an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) mode using CPUs on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To configure a Slurm script for an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) mode using CPUs, you need to load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03`. The script should specify your account, time limit, memory per core (`--mem-per-cpu`), and total number of cores (`--ntasks`). The `mapdl` command will use `-dis` for distributed memory. For the Beluga cluster, `intelmpi` is used along with `export KMP_AFFINITY=none`, while other clusters use `openmpi`. On the Cedar cluster, an additional step involves creating an `outdir-$SLURM_JOBID` directory and linking `libstdc++.so.6.0.29` to adjust the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process for submitting an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) mode with GPU acceleration to an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit an Ansys Mechanical APDL job in Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) mode with GPU acceleration, your Slurm script must load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03`. You'll need to specify your account, time limit, total memory (`--mem`), number of cores (`--tasks`), and request GPUs using `#SBATCH --gpus-per-node=1` (adjust quantity as needed). An environment variable `export ANSGPU_PRINTDEVICES=1` should be set. The `mapdl` command will include `-smp` for shared memory, `-acc nvidia` for GPU acceleration, `-na $SLURM_GPUS_ON_NODE` for the number of GPUs, and specify an output directory with `-dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID` and the input file with `-i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`. For the Cedar cluster, you also need to set `export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$EBROOTGCC/../lib/gcc`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specific cluster-dependent considerations when running Ansys Mechanical APDL jobs in Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) mode with GPUs on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: When running Ansys Mechanical APDL jobs in Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) mode with GPUs, cluster-specific configurations are crucial. For the Beluga cluster, `intelmpi` is used, and `export KMP_AFFINITY=none` must be set. For other Alliance clusters, `openmpi` is typically used. Regardless of the cluster, you must load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03`, specify GPU resources (`--gpus-per-node`), and set `export ANSGPU_PRINTDEVICES=1`. On the Cedar cluster, a specific `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` adjustment by linking `libstdc++.so.6.0.29` is also required for proper execution.
